title: Deinostigma tamianum
intro: Deinostigma tamianum, also known as the Yunnan hehheim, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Yunnan Province in southwestern China. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Deinostigma tamianum include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various patent medicines and formulations.
taxonomy: Deinostigma tamianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the African violet family. It is a small, evergreen perennial native to China. The classification of Deinostigma tamianum places it within the order Lamiales. The plant is often found in mountainous regions with high humidity and moderate temperatures.
